2138990.name: Integument
2138990.description:
It's not a game


This is an interactive sci-fi illustration of a microscope interface. In this "living illustration," you'll find patterns and shapes that reveal information about the unique lifeforms that live on the surface of your skin, also known as, integument.

A new branch in the tree of life


Forced quantum hall dating, indicates they belong to an incredibly ancient kingdom that first appeared on Earth 4.4 billion years ago. These, hada could be the bridge between single cell to what eventually became multicellular organisms like us; humans.

User ID: mematron
Personal log abstract



Raw microscope data is collected and pre-processed in the lower level systems (engine) before being sent to the top level display (hood) or stored in a database for further processing and later retrieval.
